## 1. Macro / Rates / Inflation

| Item | Materiality | Confirms/Contradicts/Novel |
|------|-------------|----------------------------|
| Fed held rates steady at 3.5-3.75% at January 2026 meeting | MEDIUM | CONFIRMS |
| Market expects pause at March meeting, strategists still see one rate cut in 2026 | MEDIUM | CONFIRMS |
| February CPI: +0.3% monthly, +2.4% year-over-year | MEDIUM | CONFIRMS |
| Annualized inflation 2.4% January, down from 2.7% December, still above Fed's 2% target | MEDIUM | CONFIRMS |
| 10-year Treasury yield ~4.16%, spread between 2-yr and 10-yr widened significantly March 11 | MEDIUM | CONFIRMS |
| 10-2 year spread: 0.58% as of March 10, 2026 | LOW | CONFIRMS |

**Signal interaction:** CONFIRMS momentum for core_equity. Fed pause with inflation moderating supports continued equity strength. Rising long-end yields reflect fiscal concerns but not immediate Fed tightening.

**LLM notes:** The Fed's January hold and market expectations for a March pause are consistent with the "higher for longer but not higher from here" narrative. Fed officials divided over future rate path per January minutes, with some seeing further cuts if inflation declines, others preferring to hold or even raise. Inflation at 2.4% is sticky but not accelerating, and the 10-2 curve steepening is fiscal/term premium driven rather than recession signal. No immediate contradiction to growth/equity momentum.

## 3. AI / Semiconductor

| Item | Materiality | Confirms/Contradicts/Novel |
|------|-------------|----------------------------|
| U.S. drafting rules requiring government approval for AI chip exports anywhere outside U.S. (Bloomberg report March 5) | HIGH | CONTRADICTS |
| Proposal would make U.S. Commerce Dept gatekeeper for every large-scale AI infrastructure buildout globally | HIGH | CONTRADICTS |
| BIS revised license review for H200 and equivalents to case-by-case (from presumption of denial), effective Jan 15, 2026 | MEDIUM | CONFIRMS |
| China cleared ByteDance, Alibaba, Tencent for 400k+ H200 units early 2026 | MEDIUM | CONFIRMS |
| Within days of Bloomberg report, China announced new AI/semiconductor subsidy program to reduce dependence on Nvidia/AMD | MEDIUM | NOVEL |

**Signal interaction:** CONTRADICTS momentum for ai_tech sleeve. The draft export control framework is materially more restrictive than prior policy and creates regulatory overhang for NVDA/AMD revenue growth. This is fresh news (March 5) not yet fully priced.

**LLM notes:** The proposed export control escalation is a **HIGH materiality CONTRADICTION** to the strong momentum signals across SOXQ, CHAT, DTCR, BOTZ, GRID. Small deployments <1000 GPUs largely unaffected, but large hyperscaler/national AI programs (10k-200k+ GPU) face most uncertainty; data center build timelines could add several months of approval delay. Policy is not yet final (comment period expected mid-2026) but the directional risk is clear. China's immediate subsidy response confirms strategic decoupling is accelerating.

**Override candidate:** SOXQ, CHAT, DTCR trades may warrant deferral or reduced sizing pending clarity on final rule publication timeline.

## Market Regime Snapshot

VIX closed 16.35 on March 11, opened 24.90 on March 12 — sharp intraday spike reflects geopolitical headline risk but remains well below stress thresholds (soft_limit trigger would be sustained 28+). 10-year yield pushed toward 4.16% as fiscal concerns and term premium rise.

**Drawdown state:** NORMAL | 0.0% from peak per tracker input.

**Regime assessment:** Moderate volatility pickup from very low levels; geopolitical noise (Iran) creating short-term chop but no systemic stress signal. Fed on hold, inflation moderating, equity momentum intact except for regulatory overhang in AI/semis. Precious metals (IAUM/SIVR) and strategic materials (URNM/COPX) continue to benefit from supply constraints and structural demand themes.
